title: Srimad Bhagavatam I HG Acharya Ratna Dasa I SB 1.8.22 I 16.10.2016
speaker: HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

segments.text: Here are some of the specific symbolical marks on the spiritual body of the personality of Godhead which distinguish his body from the bodies of all others.
They are all special features of the body of the Lord.
The Lord may appear as one of us but he is always distinct by his specific bodily features.
Srimati Kunti claims herself unfit to see the Lord because of her being a woman.

This is claimed because women, shudras, the laborer class and the dvijabandhus or the wretched descendants of the Dhyatari classes are unfit by intelligence to understand transcendental subject matter.

Such persons, although they are unfit to enter into the spiritual affairs of the Lord, can see him as the archa-vigraha who descends on the material world just to distribute favors to the fallen souls, including the above-mentioned women, shudras and dvijabandhus.
Because such fallen souls cannot see anything beyond matter, the Lord condescends to enter into each and every one of them.

The Lord is the Lord of the innumerable universes as the Garbhodakshaya Vishnu who grows a lotus stem from the lotus-like depression in the center of his transcendental abdomen and thus Brahma, the first living being in the universe, is born.
Therefore, the Lord is known as the Pankajanabhi.
The Pankajanabhi Lord accepts the archa-vigraha, his transcendental form, in different elements, namely a form within the mind, a form made of wood.
A form made of earth, a form made of metal, a form made of jewel, a form made of paint, a form drawn on sand, etc.

All such forms of the Lord are always decorated with garlands of lotus flowers and there should be a soothing atmosphere in the temple of worship to attract the burning attention of the non-devotees, always engaged in material wranglings.
The meditators worship a form within the mind.
Therefore, the Lord is merciful even to the women, shudras, and vijabandhus, provided they agree to visit the temple of worship in different forms made for them.
Such temple visitors are not idolaters, as alleged by some men with a poor fund of knowledge.
All the great acharyas established such temples of worship in all places just to favor the less intelligent.
And one should not pose himself as transcendental.
While one is actually in the category of the shudras and the women or less, one should begin to see the Lord from his lotus feet, gradually rising to his thighs, waist, chest, and face.
One should not try to look at the face of the Lord without being accustomed to seeing the lotus feet of the Lord.
Shri Matikunti, because of her being the aunt of the Lord, did not begin to see the Lord from the lotus feet.

Thus, the Lord might feel ashamed, and thus Kunti Devi, just to save a painful situation for the Lord, began to see the Lord just above his lotus feet, that is, from the waist of the Lord, gradually rising to the face and then down to the lotus feet.
In the round, everything there is in order.
So, we are discussing the 19th verse from the chapter Prayers by Queen Kunti.
So, Queen Kunti, as we have been discussing from the last time.

In the last few weeks, she is offering her heartfelt prayers to the Supreme Lord, who is already on board the chariot towards Dwaraka, and at that time, Kunti runs towards Krishna, and starts offering all these prayers.
So, today's prayer is a very beautiful prayer, where Shri Matikunti Devi is describing the features of the Lord.
She is revealing to us how the Lord looks like.
Sometimes, many times we come across people saying, the Lord has no form.
So, to defeat all those atheistic arguments, Kunti Devi is presenting a valid evidence here, and she is describing the Lord as that personality,

whose navel is lotus... has a lotus like.. depression

is marked with the symbol of lotus flower.

Let us go one by one.
The first point that Prabhupada says in the purport is he is giving us an idea that when the Supreme Lord is there,
he has some specific insignia, some specific signs that distinguish him, that distinguishes him from the people, ordinary people.
Although he may come down as one amongst us, Prabhupada says, but still he has specific symptoms or specific insignia.
And those are the things which will help us understand whether somebody is a Lord or not.
We see that very few people are interested in spirituality.
So most of them are running like a madman behind sense gratification.
But amongst those people who are little interested in spirituality, most of them are misguided.
And we all come across very frequently that so and so God has appeared in so and so place, so and so God-man.
And if you keep reading newspapers,
now and then you hear a caption self-styled God-man trapped, self-styled God-man exposed.
So what is happening is the faith in the devotees who are genuinely seeking the mercy of the Lord,
who wants to understand the nature of the Lord, they are demotivated.
They are discouraged to continue their pursuit of spirituality.
It is very important for us to know this, have this knowledge of how do we distinguish an imposter incarnation from that of the real incarnation.
This is a very important knowledge that we all need to possess as devotees.
For example, if you take the shastras, the first thing is the shastras.
We should always consult, the Prabhupada says, three things to have this knowledge of who is an imposter and who is the real incarnation.
We should hear from the Guru.
Second is Sadhu or the saintly people.
All these three things should be in unison.
It cannot be that one is contradicting the other.
For example, we sometimes hear of somebody by name Kalki has appeared now.
Then many people, thousands of people go and maybe he is charging some amount to give his darshan.

But shastras are so precise that they give us the information of when this Kalki incarnation is going to happen.
Bhagavatam says that the Kali Yuga's life span is 4,32,000 years.
Of which we have just passed 5,000 years.
That means 4,27,00,000 years yet to go.
At the end of Kali Yuga, that means after 4,27,00,000 years,
during the conjunction between Kali Yuga and Satya Yuga, Lord Kalki appears.
And he destroys all the miscreants and then leads to the fresh cycle of Satya Yuga.
And the shastras are so precise that
they even pinpoint the location at which Lord Kalki is going to appear.
Bhagavatam says Lord Kalki will appear in a place called Sambhalpur.
And it also gives us the information on his father's name is Vishnu Joshi.
Just see, what is going to happen 4,27,00,000 years ahead of time
is precisely foretold as to the father's name, location, etc.
This is the verdict of shastras.
